reasoning: As of November 9 (our cutoff), Donald Trump had just won the 2024 election and Elon Musk was deeply involved in the transition process, essentially acting as a close advisor. Trump had previously promised to appoint Musk to head a government efficiency commission. While the hype among Polymarket traders would drive the probability up, savvy participants would recognize that formal government positions require divesture from private assets due to conflict of interest laws, making it more likely that Musk's role would be structured as an external advisor. However, the phrasing "rejoin" in the question points back to Musk's 2017 advisory council roles, meaning traders could assume an external advisory role might still count as YES. Thus, on Nov 9, the market and objective likelihood hung in the balance of how formal his role would be announced. I estimate a 55% probability.
